body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents` **Service directory:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/` **Triggered by:** PR #57780 by @nick863 ## What Changed PR #57780 added the new `AgentToolboxes` client to `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents`. This includes: - New `AgentToolboxes` client class (both custom and generated) exposing CRUD operations for toolboxes - A new companion sample file `samples/Sample_Toolboxes_CRUD.md` with comprehensive, step-by-step instructions for all operations - A test-backed sample file `tests/Samples/Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs` with `#region Snippet:` tags for code extraction - An update to `README.md` adding a `### Toolboxes` subsection ## Gaps Found - **Missing client initialization step in README Toolboxes section**: The `### Toolboxes` section in the README jumps directly to `toolboxClient.CreateToolboxVersionAsync(...)` without showing how to obtain the `AgentToolboxes` client. Users must know to call `agentsClient.GetAgentToolboxes()` and, critically, that they need to attach the `FeaturePolicy("Toolboxes=V1Preview")` experimental header to the client options — this is documented only in the standalone `samples/Sample_Toolboxes_CRUD.md` but not in the README. - **Missing operations in README Toolboxes section**: Only Create and Get operations are shown. The following operations are available but not referenced in the README at all: update toolbox default version (`UpdateToolboxAsync`), list all toolbox versions (`GetToolboxVersionsAsync`), list all toolboxes (`GetToolboxesAsync`), and delete toolbox/version (`DeleteToolboxAsync`, `DeleteToolboxVersionAsync`). The standalone `samples/` file covers all of these. - **Wrong package name in Install section**: `### Install the package` shows `dotnet add package Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI --prerelease` — this is the wrong package. It should be `dotnet add package Azure.AI.Projects.Agents --prerelease`. There is also an irrelevant Cosmos DB note that belongs to a different package. - **TOC/heading mismatch**: The table of contents lists `[Prompt Agents](#prompt-agents)` but the actual section heading under `## Examples` is `## Declarative Agents`. The wrong heading level (`##` instead of `###`) also breaks the section hierarchy. - **Duplicate snippet in README**: The `Sample_Agents_CreateAgentVersionCRUD_Async` code block appears twice — once in the `Additional concepts` section and again under the `## Declarative Agents` section. - **Test sample file typo**: `tests/Samples/Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs` has a double-'o' typo (should be `Sample_ToolboxesCRUD.cs`).
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/README.md` **Edit – Fix Install section (wrong package name and irrelevant Cosmos DB note):** Replace: ```markdown ```shell dotnet add package Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI --prerelease ``` > You must have an [Azure subscription](https://azure.microsoft.com/free/dotnet/) and [Cosmos DB account](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/cosmos-db/account-overview) (SQL API). In order to take advantage of the C# 8.0 syntax, it is recommended that you compile using the [.NET Core SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) 3.0 or higher with a [language version](https://docs.microsoft.com/dotnet/csharp/language-reference/configure-language-version#override-a-default) of `latest`. It is also possible to compile with the .NET Core SDK 2.1.x using a language version of `preview`. ``` With: ```markdown ```shell dotnet add package Azure.AI.Projects.Agents --prerelease ``` ``` **Edit – Fix TOC entry `Prompt Agents` → `Declarative Agents`:** In the Table of contents, replace: ```markdown - [Prompt Agents](#prompt-agents) ``` With: ```markdown - [Declarative Agents](#declarative-agents) ``` **Edit – Fix Examples heading level for Declarative Agents:** Replace: ```markdown ## Declarative Agents ``` With: ```markdown ### Declarative Agents ``` **Edit – Remove the duplicate `Sample_Agents_CreateAgentVersionCRUD_Async` snippet** that appears for the second time in the `## Declarative Agents` section (keep only the one in `Additional concepts`). **Edit – Expand the `### Toolboxes` section** to include client initialization and the missing operations. Replace the existing `### Toolboxes` section with: ```markdown ### Toolboxes Toolboxes allow us to store tools in Azure so that they can be retrieved and used by the Agents. As for the Hosted Agent we will need to set the experimental header, but in this scenario the header is `Toolboxes=V1Preview`. We also need to disable the `AAIP001` warning. First, create the `AgentToolboxes` client by adding the experimental header policy and calling `GetAgentToolboxes()`: ```C# Snippet:Sample_CreateClient_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD ``` In the example below we create two versions of an MCP tool and save them to Azure: ```C# Snippet:Sample_CreateToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` There are two objects which help to work with Toolboxes: `ToolboxRecord` and `ToolboxVersion`. `ToolboxRecord` can be retrieved by name; it contains the default version of the Toolbox: ```C# Snippet:Sample_GetToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` The name of the Toolbox and its version allow you to get the `ToolboxVersion` object, which contains the tools that can be used by an Agent: ```C# Snippet:Sample_GetToolboxVersion_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` To update the default version of a `ToolboxRecord`: ```C# Snippet:Sample_UpdateToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` List all versions of a toolbox: ```C# Snippet:Sample_ListToolboxVersions_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` List all toolboxes: ```C# Snippet:Sample_ListToolboxes_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` Finally, delete toolbox versions and toolboxes: ```C# Snippet:Sample_DeleteToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The snippet tags already exist in the test file `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/tests/Samples/Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs`. The README placeholder blocks added in Step 1 (e.g., `` ```C# Snippet:Sample_UpdateToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async ``) will be filled in automatically by `Update-Snippets.ps1`. No manual code pasting is needed. > **Note:** If renaming the test file to fix the typo (`Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs` → `Sample_ToolboxesCRUD.cs`), update any project references accordingly. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README has all required sections, but verify them after edits: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client) — **fix wrong package name** 2. ✅ Key concepts 3. ✅ Examples — **fix heading level and TOC entry; expand Toolboxes subsection** 4. ✅ Troubleshooting 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai` — confirms README snippet placeholders are filled from test file regions 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 ai` — only needed if public API surface changed (no changes expected for this doc fix) 5. `dotnet format sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [AI Projects Agents] Docs: README Toolboxes section missing client creation and key operations
